events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights
events.description: The United Nations is raising alarm over continued drone attacks in Sudan’s Darfur region after multiple strikes reportedly killed civilians and intensified fears for communities already trapped by the brutal conflict between rival militaries.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights

events.title: 'You see love every day and journalists don't always convey that': Author Sally Hayden
events.description: A world-renowned Irish journalist and author whose reporting from conflict zones and migration routes has earned international recognition has told FRANCE 24 that in her reporting she sees love every day, and that journalists don't always convey that. Sally Hayden is a winner of the Orwell Prize, and in her new book "This is Also a Love Story" she turns away from the headlines and statistics to focus on love, as well as connection and human resilience in the middle of catastrophe. She spoke to us in Perspective.

events.title: WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness
events.description: The recent Ebola and hantavirus outbreaks demonstrate that the world is still vulnerable to rapidly spreading infectious diseases, Tedros Ghebreyesus, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), warned on Saturday at the close of the 79th World Health Assembly in Geneva.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO chief calls for urgent Ebola action and pandemic preparedness

events.title: International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak
events.description: As a deadly Ebola strain continues to spread in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), with cases confirmed in neighbouring Uganda, the UN aviation agency is urging governments and flight operators to closely follow guidelines put in place following the COVID-19 pandemic.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ International airlines urged to stick to safety measures in wake of Ebola outbreak

events.title: WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak
events.description: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warns of ‘catastrophic collision of disease and conflict’, as Uganda closes border with DRC The head of the World Health Organization has called for an immediate ceasefire in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo to help tackle the Ebola outbreak there, as Uganda closed its border with its neighbour in an effort to stop the spread. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus posted on social media that the region was in the midst of a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict with the Ebola outbreak in Ituri province outpacing the response”. Tedros said on Monday that he would travel to the DRC this week. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 WHO chief calls for DRC ceasefire to tackle Ebola outbreak

events.title: Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security
events.description: Strategic petroleum reserves are declining rapidly as countries release emergency crude supplies to cushion the economic impact of the Middle East conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Oil shortages threaten global energy security

events.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
events.description: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns

events.title: Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court
events.description: The UN International Court of Justice (ICJ) on Thursday ruled that the right to strike is protected under a core International Labour Organization (ILO) convention, in a landmark advisory opinion settling a long-running dispute between workers and employers worldwide.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Right to strike protected under key labour treaty, says UN World Court

events.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
events.description: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave

events.title: Five villagers found alive in Laos cave as search continues for two missing
events.description: Video appears to show divers discovering group sitting on a rock surrounded by flood water Rescuers have reached five of seven people who have been trapped for a week in a flooded, remote cave in Laos, after days spent navigating narrow, inundated passageways amid persistent rain. Video footage shared on social media by rescue divers showed the five men crouched together on a rocky ledge in a dark cave chamber, surrounded by muddy water. “There’s no need to cry,” one of the rescue team told the men, who first entered the cave on Wednesday. Two men remain unaccounted for. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five villagers found alive in Laos cave as search continues for two missing

events.title: Iran condemns US ceasefire 'violations' after strikes in south
events.description: Iran condemned on Thursday what it called violations by the United States after its strikes on the southern port city of Bandar Abbas, while vowing to defend its sovereignty. The ministry's spokesman Esmaeil Baqaei said Iran would "take all necessary measures to defend its national sovereignty" and condemned what he called "the threatening rhetoric of American officials against Iran and several regional countries." FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Angela Diffley tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran condemns US ceasefire 'violations' after strikes in south

events.title: Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief
events.description: The UN Charter is facing one of its gravest tests in decades, Secretary-General António Guterres told the Security Council on Tuesday, warning that wars, arms races, climate shocks and the erosion of international law are placing intense pressure on the multilateral system established to prevent a third world war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of  world order, warns UN chief

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns
events.description: The Security Council is meeting for a high-level debate on strengthening the UN-centred international system amid mounting pressure on multilateralism and deepening geopolitical divisions. Discussions are expected to focus on defending the UN Charter, reforming global governance and restoring confidence in the Security Council’s ability to respond to international crises.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Founding principles of the UN facing ‘profound strain’, Guterres warns

events.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
events.description: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

events.title: Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO
events.description: Director general of World Health Organization urges neighbouring countries to take immediate action The World Health Organization has warned that the Ebola outbreak is outpacing response efforts and countries neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) are at high risk from the disease. “We are urgently scaling up operations, but at the moment the epidemic is outpacing us,” said the WHO’s director-general, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, as he urged neighbouring countries to take immediate action. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Spread of Ebola in DRC ‘outpacing’ response efforts, warns WHO

events.title: Wild weather unleashes thunderstorms across south-east Australia, triggering flash flooding
events.description: Wet weather batters parts of Queensland, NSW and Tasmania amid warnings of heavy falls on Friday Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Wild weather more typical of summer than the last days of autumn has caused chaos in south-eastern Australia, unleashing a string of severe thunderstorms. Heavy rainfall accompanied by 500,000 lightning strikes across Queensland and New South Wales triggered widespread flash flooding, road closures and travel disruptions. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wild weather unleashes thunderstorms across south-east Australia, triggering flash flooding

events.title: US ally pledges support for Trump's push to break Iran's grip on Hormuz: 'We are ready to contribute'
events.description: Czech Foreign Minister Macinka voices strong support for Trump, defends increased European defense spending and warns Iran poses a global nuclear threat.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US ally pledges support for Trump's push to break Iran's grip on Hormuz: 'We are ready to contribute'

events.title: At least 82 killed after massive gas explosion rips through coal mine in China
events.description: A massive gas explosion ripped through a coal mine in China's Shanxi province, killing at least 82 people and hospitalizing 120 others, the AP reports.

events.title: Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk
events.description: The United Nations voiced alarm on Friday over reports of an overnight attack on a vocational school and dormitory in the town of Starobilsk in Ukraine’s Luhansk region which killed and injured multiple civilians, including children.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: UN alarmed by reports of deadly strike on dormitory in occupied Luhansk

events.title: Ukraine asks Trump for more US air defence help against Russian missile attacks
events.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has written to US President Donald Trump and Congress asking for more American-made air defence ammunition to counter intensifying Russian ballistic missile attacks, Kyiv said Wednesday. The move came after a recent escalation in aerial attacks by both sides in the more than four-year war that followed Russia’s all-out invasion of its neighbor. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine asks Trump for more US air defence help against Russian missile attacks

events.title: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
events.description: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro

events.title: Ebola epidemic spreading rapidly and outpacing containment efforts
events.description: There are more than 900 suspected cases of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and 220 suspected deaths, the head of the World Health Organization (WHO), Tedros Ghebreyesus, said on Monday.

events.title: Canada to order military plane fleet from Sweden in shift from US suppliers
events.description: Mark Carney announces purchase of Saab’s GlobalEye early warning aircraft to patrol Arctic territory Canada has announced plans to buy a fleet of early warning planes from Sweden’s Saab rather than a competing option from Boeing, as the country seeks to reduce reliance on US defense firms. Mark Carney, the prime minister, said on Wednesday that Canada would opt for Saab’s GlobalEye, which is based on Bombardier’s Global 6500 jet. Boeing’s E-7 Wedgetail plane – which has suffered from delays and cost overruns – had also been in contention. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canada to order military plane fleet from Sweden in shift from US suppliers

events.title: Five people stuck in flooded cave in Laos for week found alive
events.description: Five people who were stuck in a flooded cave in central Laos for more than a week have been found alive, but two others are still missing, rescuers say.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Five people stuck in flooded cave in Laos for week found alive

events.title: US embassy in Ukraine warns of 'potentially significant air attack' that could happen in next 24 hours
events.description: The U.S. Embassy in Kyiv issued a security alert warning of a potentially significant air attack that may occur within the next 24 hours in Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US embassy in Ukraine warns of 'potentially significant air attack' that could happen in next 24 hours

events.title: At least 16 students killed in fire at Kenya girls school dorm
events.description: Education minister says 16 killed, 79 injured at Utumishi Girls School in Gilgil, central Kenya.

events.title: Bolivia at 'breaking point,' president warns protesters
events.description: President Rodrigo Paz warned Wednesday that Bolivia was at a "breaking point" after nearly a month of protests that have caused shortages of food, fuel and life-saving medicine. The US-backed Paz, who took office six months ago amid the worst economic crisis here in four decades, is battling a groundswell of fury over his center-right policies. The political capital La Paz has been besieged by low-income workers and members of the majority Indigenous majority calling for his resignation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Bolivia at 'breaking point,' president warns protesters

events.title: Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’
events.description: The overnight Russian attack on Ukraine, which centred on the capital, Kyiv, reportedly involved some 90 missiles, including a powerful hypersonic ballistic missile and 60 drones. The senior UN official in the country called for an end to civilian harm.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Massive Russian strike on Kyiv: ‘I hope my friends are alive’

events.title: World Urban Forum backs ‘Baku Call to Action’ on global housing crisis
events.description: A landmark gathering of more than 57,000 participants – the largest in the history of the World Urban Forum – closed on Friday in Baku with an urgent call to rethink how the world houses its people, as a new roadmap urges governments, cities and communities to act collectively on a crisis affecting billions.

events.title: Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official
events.description: The Security Council is meeting at the request of Russia which on Friday accused Ukraine of targeting a student dormitory overnight in the occupied Luhansk region, reportedly killing six people – including children – and injuring dozens. Kyiv has denied targeting the civilian building, saying it had struck a Russian military drone command headquarters, according to news reports. Follow live coverage below and for full meetings coverage of the council, click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Security Council LIVE: Human cost of Ukraine war, a pattern that ‘defies’ international law, says senior UN aid official

events.title: Ebola risk ‘very high’ in eastern DR Congo as UN intensifies response
events.description: The UN is rushing emergency personnel, funding and supplies into eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) to counter the fast-growing Ebola outbreak spreading through conflict-ravaged provinces.

events.title: Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23
events.description: At least 23 killed and 70 wounded after a suicide bomber reportedly detonated an explosives-laden vehicle alongside an incoming passenger train in Quetta, Pakistan.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23

events.title: Lebanon: Massive fireball as Israel strikes city of Tyre
events.description: A massive fireball was seen rising into the air Thursday as heavy Israeli strikes pounded Lebanon's southern coastal city of Tyre. The strikes, which come as Muslims celebrate the holiday of Eid al-Adha, followed a warning by Israel that it had declared a new swathe of southern Lebanon a combat zone and told residents to evacuate.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Lebanon: Massive fireball as Israel strikes city of Tyre

events.title: Germany news: Health care staff protest plan for major cuts
events.description: Medical staff in Germany are protesting proposed cuts that ministers say are necessary to stop spiraling costs. Meanwhile, a World War II bomb meant thousands were evacuated from their homes. DW has more.

events.title: Sixteen pupils killed in Kenya school fire
events.description: Dozens of people were also injured in the fire, the cause of which is not yet known.

events.title: Greetings from Cape Verde, where the sounds of samba, jazz and morna fill the air
events.description: Music is interwoven with the sounds of daily life in this West African island nation, which hosted two international music festivals in April and has been named the African Capital of Culture for 2028.

events.title: North Korea: New report sheds light on chemical weapons
events.description: A new study shows the North Korean regime is investing heavily in chemical weapons capacity, with analysts warning they could be deployed by the regime if it faces an existential threat.

events.title: Crises in Amhara, Tigray cloud Ethiopia's election 2026
events.description: The June 1 elections come as the country debates federalism, governance and national unity in polls that could define the country's democratic future.

events.title: North Korea says it tested new warheads, technology and navigation in latest launches

events.title: President Sheinbaum allows Iran team to stay in Mexico during World Cup after US refusal
events.description: Fifa approached Mexico after US declined to host Iran squad despite it playing group games in the United States Mexico’s president, Claudia Sheinbaum , said on Monday her government agreed to allow the Iranian national football team to stay in Mexico during the World Cup , adding that the US did not want to host the team. The team will still play its group stage matches in the US but its base has been moved to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego, California, a move that Iran’s football federation announced recently and that was confirmed by Fifa, the sport’s governing body. Continue reading...

events.title: U.S. strikes Iran again. And, DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades
events.description: The U.S. says it has struck Iran again as peace talks continue to end the conflict. And, the federal government has charged a Google staffer for allegedly using insider information in Polymarket trades.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 U.S. strikes Iran again. And, DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades

events.title: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
events.description: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

events.title: Oil prices jump after US launches new attacks on Iran
events.description: The strikes come despite a ceasefire between Tehran and Washington as the two countries hold peace talks.

events.title: The race for oil: will Jamaica be the next country to drill and what does that mean for its green pledges?
events.description: With early tests suggesting the presence of crude oil, the Caribbean island has begun to debate whether it could justify becoming a producer Jamaica is closer than ever to drilling for oil. Tests on samples from the seabed off the Caribbean island’s south coast earlier this year identified hydrocarbons , which suggest the presence of crude oil below ground. Jamaica imports all its fuel, which costs about $1.5-2bn (£1.1bn-1.5bn) annually , depending on global oil prices. It is a persistent drag on an economy that generated $4.3bn from tourism, its biggest earner, in 2024 . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 The race for oil: will Jamaica be the next country to drill and what does that mean for its green pledges?

events.title: Distrust, conflict hamper Congo's Ebola response
events.description: The epicenter of the Ebola outbreak is in Mongbwalu, a poor gold-mining town of 130,000 people, in Ituri province, in eastern Congo.

events.title: Israel strikes Beirut southern suburb ahead of crucial Washington talks
events.description: Israel’s air force carried out an airstrike on a southern suburb of the capital, Beirut, Thursday afternoon, the Israeli military said, further straining a fragile ceasefire a day ahead of crucial negotiations in Washington. The strike hit an apartment building but it was not immediately clear who might have been targeted. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis reports from Beirut, Lebanon.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel strikes Beirut southern suburb ahead of crucial Washington talks

events.title: Zelensky says Ukraine needs US antiballistic missiles
events.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky urged US President Donald Trump and the Congress to supply Patriot PAC-3 missiles and additional air defence systems to protect against Russian ballistic missiles in a letter obtained by The Associated Press, warning that deliveries are falling dangerously short as Russia escalates its missile campaign.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Zelensky says Ukraine needs US antiballistic missiles

events.title: Khalida Popal and Javier Zanetti among football stars appointed UN Champions

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Khalida Popal and Javier Zanetti among football stars appointed UN Champions

events.title: Family accuse FCDO of lack of support over death of UK man in Grenada
events.description: Pathologist found Andrew Frederick’s death was a homicide but UK authorities refused specialist help The grieving family of a British man found dead in his home on the Caribbean island of Grenada have accused UK authorities of failing to support their fight for justice. The family of Andrew Frederick, 47, whose body was discovered on 4 January, are calling for an urgent review of the policies governing UK assistance to the loved ones of Britons killed abroad. Continue reading...

events.title: Argentina’s ‘European’ self-image under renewed scrutiny after racist incidents in Brazil
events.description: Case involving seven-year-old boy is latest flashpoint in debate over race relations in Latin America ‘Argentina needs to end its fantasy of being a European country’: Lucrecia Martel on the story of a killing A woman celebrating her 32nd birthday on a train journey in the Brazilian state of Minas Gerais was horrified when a fellow passenger alerted her that an unknown man had been secretly filming her seven-year-old son. When confronted, the man – an Argentinian tourist – initially refused to show his phone. But after being pressed by other travellers, the man admitted he had sent the images to a WhatsApp contact. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Argentina’s ‘European’ self-image under renewed scrutiny after racist incidents in Brazil

events.title: Iran and US trade air strikes after Trump dismisses report of Hormuz deal
events.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard targeted a US airbase on Thursday after the US military carried out what a Washington official said were strikes on an Iranian drone operation near the Strait of Hormuz, hours after President Donald Trump rejected a report he was close to a compromise deal with Tehran. The escalation in hostilities highlighted threats ​to the tenuous ceasefire between ‌the US and Iran that took effect in early April, dampening hopes for a peace deal and sending oil prices surging again.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran and US trade air strikes after Trump dismisses report of Hormuz deal

events.title: Gaza: Life-saving medicines blocked as killing continues, disease gains ground

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Samsung memory chip staff in line for £310,000 bonuses after AI profit-sharing deal
events.description: Agreement averts strike and shows latest impact of AI boom as two more chipmakers join $1tn club Employees at Samsung Electronics’ memory chip division are to receive bonuses averaging about £310,000 each through a landmark profit-sharing agreement, as the AI boom drives up chipmakers’ profits. Fears of a strike at Samsung were averted on Wednesday after two unions for the world’s largest memory chipmaker said 74% of the 62,616 workers who cast their votes had backed the deal. events.title: From Kharkiv to the UK’s health service: Ukrainian doctor rebuilds her life – and helps others do the same
events.description: On 23 February 2022, in her home city of Kharkiv, Dr Inna Soldatenko finished work, collected her daughter from school, cooked dinner and prepared a lecture for her students. The next morning, she awoke to explosions.

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: Veterans turn war stories into music at Trump Kennedy Center in powerful patriotic showcase
events.description: CreatiVets brought veteran musicians to the Trump Kennedy Center's Millennium Stage for an emotional showcase blending combat stories with live music.

events.title: Israel eliminates head of Hamas' military wing in Gaza strike
events.description: Israeli forces killed Mohammed Odeh, the new head of Hamas' military wing, in an airstrike in northern Gaza, according to the Israel Defense Forces.

events.title: Iran signals ‘mass sacrifice’ in 'high stakes' Saddam-era warning amid Trump deal talks

events.title: Man arrested after three injured in stabbing at Swiss train station
events.description: The suspect, a 31-year-old Swiss man, has been arrested, police say, as areas around the station are cordoned off.

events.title: Iran says US must issue multiple-entry visas for its World Cup squad
events.description: Iran's delegation will be based in Tijuana, Mexico, and will travel to the US for all three group stage matches.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Ukraine: EU's Kallas warns against Russian mediator 'trap'
events.description: The European Union's top diplomat, Kaja Kallas, has warned member states against falling into a Russian "trap" in future negotiations on Ukraine as the bloc's foreign ministers meet in Cyprus. DW has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: EU's Kallas warns against Russian mediator 'trap'

events.title: Puerto Rico governor announces emergency to fight worsening coastal erosion
events.description: Puerto Rico’s governor has announced a state of emergency following critical coastal erosion along the U.S. territory’s north coast

events.title: Review of landmark nuclear treaty breaks up without consensus, raising arms race fears
events.description: After four weeks of negotiations at UN Headquarters in New York, the 11th Review Conference of the nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty (NPT) ended on Friday without consensus on a final declaration.

events.title: Paris neighbourhoods: The underrated 14th arrondissement
events.description: In this edition of French Connections, Genie Godula and Florence Villeminot continue their tour of Paris with a stop in the most underrated district of the capital: the 14th arrondissement. Calm and residential, most tourists won't make the journey to this southern edge of the Left Bank, but it's definitely worth a trip if you want a delightful taste of behind-the-scenes Paris. Here you'll find a village feel with cosy streets, hidden alleys and quirky sights... not to mention 6 million skeletons beneath your feet in the Paris Catacombs! Join us as we wander around the quatorzième arrondissement.

events.title: Europe heatwave 'brutal reminder' of climate change
events.description: The UN climate chief said Wednesday that a record-breaking early heatwave scorching a swathe of western Europe was "a brutal reminder of the spiraling impacts of the climate crisis". Britain and France have reported their hottest ever May days this week as a "heat dome" brought sizzling temperatures more typical of midsummer to western Europe. With temperatures remaining stubbornly above 30C across much of northern Europe and predicted to go up again Thursday, UN climate chief Simon Stiell said the "main culprit" was humanity's burning of coal, oil and gas -- the primary driver of climate change.

events.title: Federal agents in New Jersey beat back anti-ICE agitators in chaos outside Delaney Hall detention facility
events.description: Homeland Security Secretary Mullin says ICE officers were assaulted by anti-ICE rioters who sprayed law enforcement with an unknown chemical substance.

events.title: Australia sues US giant 3M over 'forever chemicals' in firefighting foam
events.description: The A$2bn case, which centres on contamination at defence sites, is the largest ever brought by the government.

events.title: Brazil World Cup 2026 team preview: Players to watch, group matches, squad
events.description: While one of the least fancied Brazilian World Cup sides of all time, Ancelotti's squad is still loaded with talent.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Brazil World Cup 2026 team preview: Players to watch, group matches, squad

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Iran turns internet back on, but old restrictions remain
events.description: Communication with the outside world remains difficult in Iran, even after an 88-day total internet blockade was lifted. The blackout has had significant economic and social consequences.

events.title: Ethiopia votes amid geopolitical rivalries in Horn of Africa
events.description: Ethiopia will hold its seventh national election on June 1, 2026. The vote takes place as Gulf states compete for influence in the region and the rivalry between Ethiopia and Egypt over the Nile intensifies.

events.title: India news: Siddaramaiah steps down as Karnataka CM
events.description: Main headlines in India include DK Shivkumar being set to replace Siddaramaiah as chief minister for the southern state Karnataka, and Muslims celebrating Eid al-Adha across the country. DW has more.

events.title: ‘I felt my humanity was bastardised’: Cynthia Erivo says reaction to Ariana Grande red carpet incident rooted in racism
events.description: Wicked co-star said reactions to the incident, which included suggestions she was Grande’s ‘bodyguard’, reflect an insidious view of Black women Wicked star Cynthia Erivo has said that reactions to the incident at the Singapore premiere of Wicked: For Good, in which she stepped in to fend off a red-carpet invader who grabbed co-star Ariana Grande, revealed “the insidious nature of how we view Black women” and put her off campaigning for Oscars. In an interview with Variety , Erivo said that she and Grande were “terrified” when Johnson Wen jumped a barrier at Universal Studios Singapore and rushed towards them. “Nobody moved. Nobody moved. So I moved because my brain went, ‘Get him away! Get him out of here!’ … And what people couldn’t see is that he wouldn’t let go [of Grande]. He wouldn’t let go. So I just kept pushing at him to get him off.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘I felt my humanity was bastardised’: Cynthia Erivo says reaction to Ariana Grande red carpet incident rooted in racism

events.title: US firm fined for Australian immigration security failures. How much? Border force refuses to say
events.description: Gavan Reynolds cites ‘commercial-in-confidence nature of the contract’ in declining to answer Greens senator’s question in parliament Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The US private prison company running Australian immigration detention centres was slapped with penalties by the Australian government after allowing a dozen escapes, but the head of the Australian Border Force has declined to reveal the cost of the punishment. Greens senator David Shoebridge was scathing of what he called “a disturbing set of reports” about private prison company MTC and its local subsidiary Secure Journeys, revealed in a Guardian Australia investigation this week . Continue reading...

events.title: Palestinians use recycling as Israel's restrictions trigger a trash crisis
events.description: Palestinians in the West Bank live amid garbage following Israeli restrictions. Two Palestinian entrepreneurs are trying to make a change.

events.title: Why the French Open is named after Roland Garros, who didn't play tennis
events.description: Roland Garros, a WWI-era pilot, is credited with revolutionizing aerial combat. The major Paris tennis tournament was named after him in 1928, a decade after his death in combat.

events.title: Kai Havertz: Arsenal and Germany's man for the big moment

events.title: Talk to Russia? First, Europe needs to pick an envoy – and know what it wants to say
events.description: With the US bogged down in its war against Iran, Ukraine has been urging Europe to step up and play an active role in negotiations on bringing the Russian invasion to an end. But EU officials say the bloc first needs to be aligned on what it wants to say to Moscow – and who would be best placed to serve as a mediator.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Talk to Russia? First, Europe needs to pick an envoy – and know what it wants to say

events.title: 'The next step for humanity as a whole': China’s space race with the US heats up
events.description: NASA is ordering landers, rovers and drones for a sprawling moon base, less than two months after the Artemis II's record-breaking lunar flyaround. The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four US companies. And the Americans are not alone. On Sunday, the Chinese space agency launched Shenzhou-23 which will see an astronaut orbit the earth for a year. Its ambitions include having their own manned presence on the moon by 2035. For more, FRANCE 24’s Sharon Gaffney is joined by the 'Godmother of Space Law', Dr Michelle LD Hanlon, executive director of the Centre for Air and Space Law at the University of Mississippi.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'The next step for humanity as a whole': China’s space race with the US heats up

events.description: A man stabbed and wounded three people at a Swiss train station, police said.

events.title: ‘She does not back down’: the couple seeking to legalise same-sex marriage in Botswana
events.description: Bonolo Selelo and Tsholofelo Kumile are going to court for right to wed but face fierce opposition from church groups Bonolo Selelo was at Botswana’s national museum for a Gaborone Pride event when she spotted Tsholofelo Kumile and was struck by her good looks. The two initiated a conversation and when Kumile expressed anxiety about what a tarot reading at the event might hold, Selelo thought nothing of offering her a hug. The reading turned out positive but Kumile claimed her hug anyway and they talked for hours. That was 1 October 2023. Two months later, they moved in together. Then, on a hike during the Easter holidays in 2024, Selelo proposed to Kumile. A year later, they visited a local government office to register their intent to marry and were told it wasn’t legal. Continue reading...

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
events.description: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

events.title: Modelling shows 90% of young Australians will be better off under Labor’s tax reforms
events.description: The $1,000 tax deduction, $250 ‘working Australians tax offset’, and CGT and negative gearing changes will benefit most young people, Treasury secretary says Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ninety per cent of young Australians will be better off under the Albanese government’s tax proposals, the Treasury claims, as Labor moves to pass its reforms into law. The government introduced the tax changes to parliament on Thursday before a heated question time in which the opposition leader, Angus Taylor, called Anthony Albanese an “arrogant prick”, while separately the Nationals demanded the government call an early election. Continue reading...

events.title: UK re-chalks Dorset's famed and feisty Cerne Abbas Giant
events.description: Britain's National Trust is working on replacing the chalk outline of the Cerne Abbas Giant in Dorset. The Saxon-era carving depicts a club-wielding man "still standing tall," as the charity puts it.

events.title: Ethiopian PM Abiy Ahmed's party holds final rally before polls
events.description: In less than one week, Ethiopians will head to the polls for the country's general elections. Prime minister Abiy Ahmed and the ruling Prosperity Party are a shoo-in to be re-elected. But opposition figures say that the necessary pre-conditions for a democratic vote are not in place. FRANCE 24's correspondent Tom Canetti attended the party’s last political campaign before next week's vote.

events.title: Berlin police arrest man suspected of being an accomplice to Holocaust Memorial stabbing
events.description: An alleged accomplice of a man who was convicted of stabbing and seriously wounding a Spanish tourist at Berlin’s Holocaust Memorial last year has been arrested in the German capital

events.title: Suspect in viral monk seal harassment video says he was doxxed as federal court fight begins
events.description: Tourist Igor Lytvynchuk allegedly threw a rock at a Hawaiian monk seal in Maui and now faces federal harassment charges in court.

events.title: China deployed over 100 vessels near Taiwan in the wake of Trump-Xi summit, Taiwan security official claims
events.description: China deployed over 100 vessels around Taiwan after the Trump-Xi Beijing summit, Taiwan National Security Council Secretary General Joseph Wu says.

events.title: Dormitory fire at Kenyan girls’ school kills at least 16 students
events.description: Parents face anxious wait for updates after blaze tears through Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county A fire has ripped through a dormitory at a girls’ school in Kenya’s Rift valley, killing at least 16 students. The fire broke out just after midnight at Utumishi girls academy in Gilgil, Nakuru county, about 76 miles north-west of Nairobi, according to police. Continue reading...

events.title: US building Ebola quarantine center in Kenya for Americans amid outbreak
events.description: Some experts criticize White House approach and say not allowing Americans to return to US hurts treatment efforts The Trump administration is building a quarantine and treatment center in Kenya for Americans affected by the Ebola outbreak, instead of bringing them home. The White House on Wednesday confirmed that the US was setting up a facility in Kenya for Americans to quarantine after Ebola exposure in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). Continue reading...

events.title: EU fines Temu for failing to stop sale of illegal and dangerous products
events.description: European Commission finds shoppers on Chinese website very likely to find unsafe items and imposes €200m penalty EU regulators have fined the Chinese shopping website Temu €200m (£173m) for failing to stop the sale of illegal and dangerous products. The European Commission imposed the penalty after a 19-month investigation that found consumers were very likely to encounter illegal or unsafe products including baby toys and electronics on the firm’s website. Continue reading...

events.title: Canada to buy Swedish surveillance plane over US models
events.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney says the Canadian Armed Forces need the plane to detect and deter threats across the Arctic.

events.title: 'The Arab Spring was something from the people for the people': Singer Emel Mathlouthi
events.description: The woman whose song was at the heart of the Arab Spring has told FRANCE 24 that even 15 years later she looks back on her role, and the role of the protesters with admiration. Tunisian-American artist Emel Mathlouthi's song Kelmti Horra, or "My Word is Free", became something of an anthem at the time as uprisings against the authorities sprang up in one county after another. This weekend she's in concert here in Paris, and she joined us beforehand in Perspective.

events.title: Canadian teen girl charged with arson after allegedly torching American baseball team's charter bus
events.description: A Canadian teen is charged with arson after allegedly setting fire to a U.S. baseball team's charter bus during a road trip to Winnipeg, police say.

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Iranians are back online after a monthslong shutdown but face heavy restrictions
events.description: Iranians began to regain internet access after authorities ended a monthslong shutdown. Users said service was slow and spotty in some areas, with apps like YouTube and Instagram heavily restricted.

events.description: An Australian woman has been charged with traveling to Syria and joining the Islamic State.

events.title: Nebraska K-9 sniffs out 525 pounds of cocaine during routine traffic stop, leading to California man's arrest

events.title: Jill Biden says she thought husband was having a stroke during 2024 debate
events.description: The former US first lady told CBS News that she was frightened by Joe Biden's performance against Donald Trump.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: DOJ launches criminal probe into E. Jean Carroll — reports
events.description: The US Justice Department has launched a criminal inquiry into former magazine writer E. Jean Carroll, who accused Donald Trump of sexually assaulting her at a department store 30 years ago.

events.title: Brazil to revive highway project through Amazon amid concerns
events.description: Brazil will invest $75 million to pave a highway that cuts through the center of the Amazon rainforest. Environmentalists argue for strong protections to conserve an ecosystem so crucial to curbing climate change.

events.title: Concerns grow over conditions at ICE facility in New Jersey
events.description: Democratic members of Congress have toured the Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention center, which has become a focus point for opponents of US President Donald Trump's migration agenda.

events.title: I. Coast faces sheep shortage ahead of Tabaski amid Sahel trade disruptions
events.description: Just days before the Islamic festival of Tabaski, livestock markets in Abidjan are slowing down. Burkina Faso and Mali have suspended exports, while insecurity and the closure of Sahel trade corridors are disrupting traditional supply routes. The result: sheep are becoming scarce and prices are soaring across Abidjan’s markets. Our team on the ground reports.

events.title: How Norwegian researchers are using satellite images to track tourism’s environmental impact
events.description: Norway’s northern Lofoten Islands are attracting an increasing number of tourists, raising concerns about the environmental impact. To help document these effects, Norwegian researchers have launched a new project that utilises satellite images.

events.title: Mother, boyfriend allegedly abandoned blindfolded young sons in remote forest as part of 'game': reports
events.description: Marine Rousseau and Marc Ballabriga allegedly abandoned her two young children in a forest in Europe, blindfolding them as part of a so-called game.

events.title: ‘Designated target’ Mojtaba Khamenei to sign Trump deal in ‘unprecedented’ courier setup
events.description: Experts say the U.S. is negotiating with an invisible counterparty as Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly remains in hiding and relies on secret couriers.

events.title: The kingmakers of Makerfield: English town braces for crucial by-election
events.description: On June 18, voters will determine the fate of Andy Burnham, a possible contender to replace embattled PM Starmer.

events.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
events.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

events.title: Greener pasture of a shepherd’s life lures Chinese workers penned in by ‘996’ jobs
events.description: When an Inner Mongolia farmer sought two herders to tend his 3,000 sheep, he was swamped with applicants including graduates, factory labourers and white-collar workers A Chinese farm owner’s recruitment drive for shepherds has ended in success after his job advert seeking people to work on his Inner Mongolia ranch went viral, drawing the attention of city dwellers struggling to find work and highlighting growing strains in China’s labour market. Zuo Xiaoyong posted an advert on Chinese social media in late April seeking two shepherds, preferably a couple, to take 3,000 sheep out to graze on a 2,000ha pasture in the summer. The shepherds would also undertake indoor feeding and cleaning during the winter when temperatures can drop below -30C at his ranch roughly 300km from Xilinhot city, near the Mongolian border. Continue reading...

events.title: 'Cosmonauts', 'Rivals' and Nicolas Cage's TV debut
events.description: Critic Dheepthika Laurent unpacks this month's TV releases, including the gripping Soviet space race drama told from the Russian side, "Star City", the return of the outrageously scandalous 1980s hit "Rivals" and Nicolas Cage stepping into his very first TV role in "Spider Noir".

events.title: France famous 'Yellow train' is back
events.description: It's a 63 kilometre railway running between two villages in the Catalan region of the French Pyrenees, offering spectacular views along the way. France's famous "Yellow train" is now back in service after being shut down for months because of a landslide. The more than century old rail line attracts thousands of tourists each year, carrying passengers on a nostalgic journey through the mountains.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ France famous 'Yellow train' is back

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: Victorian greyhound trainer avoids ban despite dog being found with cocaine in system
events.description: Tribunal decision on Geoffrey Dalton comes as advocacy group releases data showing 30 greyhounds have died at or after Victorian races this year Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A veteran greyhound trainer whose dog was found to have cocaine in its system at a race has avoided a temporary ban despite the industry regulator calling for one. The Victorian Civil and Administrative Tribunal (Vcat) on Thursday decided not to impose tougher penalties on Geoffrey Dalton, who was fined $300 after the dog tested positive for cocaine before a 2024 race. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Victorian greyhound trainer avoids ban despite dog being found with cocaine in system

events.title: Treasury secretary says ‘no clear evidence’ for CGT reform criticisms – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Scams come in all shapes and sizes (and none of them are nice), and the government is considering creating rules that would force banks, telcos and digital platforms to automatically reimburse victims of smaller scams of up to $3,000. Labor is considering a range of options as part of a scam protection framework. For smaller losses, $3,000 and under, what we’re proposing is that there should be automatic payment to consumers where they can verify that there has been a scam. Scams that get into the six figures: some investment scams, some romance scams. And that’s where dispute resolution processes would come into play. What we want to do is to make sure that we don’t have the wrong incentives for perpetrators to see Australia as a soft target. But the balance is that with very small claims we don’t want to have processes that are completely disproportionate to the value of the sum in dispute. Taxpayer funds should never be used for private business, nor were they in this case. When we’re undertaking whether it be community engagement, media engagement, stakeholder meetings, you know, assisting colleagues, there are arrangements in place to assist us to do that work that is right and appropriate, and it is not right and appropriate to bill the taxpayer, as you say, for personal activities. And I did not do that. Yep. Continue reading...

events.title: In Colombia, a flashy lawyer touts his tough-on-crime approach in presidential race
events.description: In Colombia's election this Sunday, a flamboyant criminal lawyer — who flaunts his wealth, machismo and even his music videos — is pitching himself as the toughest candidate on crime.

events.title: A look inside Japan's largest prison for women
events.description: Japan has recently carried out reforms to its prison system, but inside Tochigi, its biggest women's prison, strict discipline and tough conditions still define daily life.
events.ticker_label: 💥 A look inside Japan's largest prison for women

events.title: US: Former CIA officer charged with stealing gold bars from government
events.description: A former CIA official with top secret-level clearance is accused of stealing gold bars worth millions from the US government and stashing them in his home. Investigators also seized foreign currency and luxury watches.

events.title: New Ebola cases in Congo: What you need to know
events.description: The virus spreads from direct person-to-person contact. But here's what makes it especially lethal: it persists in corpses, and funeral practices often take place precisely when bodies are most infectious.

events.title: Russia looks to forced mobilisation after losing 500,000 soldiers in Ukraine
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Thursday, May 28, 2026: Papers discuss whether Russia could potentially invade Europe. But first, Spanish front pages cover the latest developments in the political corruption scandal that has hit the Socialist Party. Elsewhere, US President Donald Trump's renovation of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool continues to make headlines. Finally, some dogs are fashionable, but one pup is in the dog house.

events.title: Germany's most-wanted woman, 67, jailed after decades on run
events.description: A former member of the notorious Baader-Meinhof gang has been jailed for a string of armed robberies after decades on the run.

events.title: Brazil is set to join other Latin American countries with a 40-hour, 5-day workweek
events.description: Brazil was set to join other Latin American countries that have shortened working hours as the lower house approved a constitutional amendment establishing a 40-hour, five-day workweek

events.title: Brazil to invest $75 million in highway through Amazon and unveils environmental protection plan

events.title: Video shows illegal aliens found crammed inside semi-truck sleeper cab in alleged smuggling bust

events.title: Israeli ambassador compares France's far-left leader's rhetoric to Hitler as antisemitism surges

events.title: Mass tanker blackout rattles Gulf ahead of 1.35M-barrel oil transfer amid US-Iran talks: firm
events.description: Maritime tracking signals collapse near the UAE's Fujairah oil hub hours before President Trump announced progress on a bilateral peace deal with Iran, according to an AI maritime firm.
